result.status_detail: INTERCEPT_RESTORED -- newChat 200 SSE (started/data/stopped), /api/chat NOT fired, UI blank (envelope payload shape mismatch)
console_errors: 4 console (likely React Tooltip warnings + downstream effects of Stage 2 not firing)
result.rendered_text_in_chat_area: (empty -- no user bubble, no AI bubble, only Mistral logo + Ask-anything input)
result.comparison_to_602: Intercept FULLY RETURNED after path revert: newChat now serves the 0.9kB synthetic SSE envelope (started/data/stopped) and the client suppresses Stage 2 /api/chat. Opposite of #602 which let real upstream complete and stream PII analysis (25.3KB).
result.implication_for_engine_hypothesis: CONFIRMS the path-narrowing to '/api/chat' (Stage 2, no PII text) was the SOLE bypass cause for #599/#602. Root path '/' restores intercept on Stage 1 (newChat) where prompt PII is detectable. Engine intercept layer is HEALTHY. QUIC/cert/intercept-rule-deletion theories all REFUTED. Remaining work: envelope payload shape inside data frame must match what Le Chat client subscribes to for streaming text rendering.
result.diagnostic_for_dev_team: Cycle 94 R-23 ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED. The bypass observed in #599 and #602 was caused entirely by APF path narrowing to '/api/chat' which carries no PII text (Stage 2 only delivers stream; prompt is in Stage 1 newChat per #594 ground truth). With path reverted to '/', APF intercepts newChat correctly: 200 OK + text/event-stream + 0.9kB synthetic envelope (started/data/stopped 3-frame triplet) observed in DevTools EventStream. Critically, the Le Chat client RESPECTS the synthetic termination and does NOT fire Stage 2 /api/chat at all -- so user gets no PII analysis even though our payload doesn't render a warning. This is acceptable failure mode (PII protected, just no visible warning text). Next iteration: enrich tRPC SSE data frame with proper SuperJSON-wrapped message structure containing chatId / messageId / contentChunks[0].text per #597 ground truth, so the warning text actually renders in the chat bubble. Recommend mistral_trpc_sse_v2 (path stays '/', body shape upgraded). Critical hypothesis ratification: keep PATH at '/' for all future mistral templates -- never narrow to '/api/chat'.

screenshots_inventory.description: Final chat UI state with DevTools closed -- empty (no bubble, no warning, no error, no PII analysis)
Chat UI mid-test with DevTools open showing newChat response Headers (Content-Type: text/event-stream, 200 OK)
Network panel -- only message.newChat?batch=1 (200, h2, 0.9kB, 38ms); EventStream shows 3 frames started/data/stopped
Verbatim newChat response body -- the APF-served 0.9kB SSE envelope (started/data/stopped triplet)
Diagnostic note: /api/chat (Stage 2) was NOT fired this run -- client respected synthetic termination from newChat

result.next_iteration_recommendation: Build mistral_trpc_sse_v2: path STAYS '/', body upgraded to include SuperJSON-wrapped message with chatId (extracted from request body) + synthetic messageId + contentChunks[0].text containing warning text. Reference #597 captured 29-field message structure for the data frame payload. Goal: render warning text in chat bubble while preserving the intercept benefits demonstrated here.
obstacles_encountered: DevTools 'Response' tab said 'Nothing to preview' for SSE -- had to use EventStream tab to confirm 3-frame triplet
Data frame inner JSON was truncated in DevTools list view; payload identifiable as tRPC data.json wrapper but inner data.j... not fully extractable without expanding row
Initial page load showed stale rendered PII response from prior #602 run (6:01pm timestamp); navigated to fresh /chat to start clean test

notes: Cycle 94 R-23 mistral path REVERT verify: **INTERCEPT_RESTORED**. Path narrowing from '/' to '/api/chat' was the SOLE cause of #599/#602 bypass (Stage 2 has no PII text per #594 ground truth). Path reverted to '/' restored Stage 1 newChat intercept: 200 OK, text/event-stream, 0.9kB synthetic SSE envelope (started/data/stopped triplet). Critically Stage 2 /api/chat NOT fired at all -- client respected termination -- so PII protected even though warning text not rendered. UI blank (no warning, no Error 6002, no PII analysis). QUIC/cert/intercept-rule-deletion theories all REFUTED. Engine intercept layer HEALTHY. Next: mistral_trpc_sse_v2 with proper SuperJSON message body (per #597) inside data frame so warning renders. CRITICAL: keep PATH at '/' for all future mistral templates. 5 artifacts in files/603/. Processed via Subagent Dispatch (7m17s, 62 tool uses).
